spec_match.rationale: The page still demonstrates real :playing, :paused, and :muted selectors on an audio element, with the visual layer driven by CSS rather than class toggles.

interactive.rationale: The silent placeholder source was replaced with an in-page generated WAV via Web Audio with a PCM fallback, but chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable for audible playback verification.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The copy now accurately says the audio is generated in-page and playable, while the CSS explanation separates Web Audio source generation from pseudo-class visuals.

edge_cases.rationale: The implementation handles missing OfflineAudioContext with a generated fallback WAV and reports audio load errors, but it still needs browser testing for autoplay policy, decoding, and audible output.

references.rationale: The page includes ChromeStatus, MDN, and Selectors Level 4 references.

design_system.rationale: The new status text uses existing typography and design variables without adding raw color styling.

openQuestions.title: Verify generated audio with chrome-devtools-mcp
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 150+ to open /v150/media-element-pseudo-classes/media-mood-board/, wait for the generated audio status, press play, confirm audible output, verify :playing/:paused/:muted visual changes, and inspect console/network.

summary: Static repair removed the silent data-URI audio and now generates a playable WAV in-page, while keeping media pseudo-class visuals CSS-driven. Browser/audio verification remains blocked because chrome-devtools-mcp is not exposed in this session.
